stepDescription: Create a merchant for in-store
tags: Two-letter ISO country code, in uppercase e.g 'SE', 'DK', 'NO'.
Provide the corporate ID of the respective merchant.
The legal name of the organization. Mandatory for a PF partner.
Merchant Category Code (MCC) of the organization. Mandatory for a PF partner.
The address of the organization. Mandatory for a PF partner.
Name of the addressee that is accepting the correspondence for the intended recipient.
City where the organisation is located.
Two-letter ISO country code in uppercase, representing the country where the organization is located.
Contact number of the organisation in code and number format.
International dialing code identifying the country or region.
Email address of the organization. Mandatory for a PF partner.
The multi merchant ID in case this merchant should belong to a multiMerchant setup.
These fields allow you to control the behavior of the API by adding additional actions or controlling the default execution path of the API call.
Allows the customization of product display for onboarding merchants. Specify a set of Product IDs (e.g. terminals, accessories) to be shown.
Represents the product ID of a product selected for delivery to the merchant.

A list of strings specifying the billing plan for a merchant. This allows you to set up a plan based on the monthly price for a terminal and the duration in months. The specific plan details are provided by Surfboard.
Configuration for acquirer settings. This is mandatory for PF partners and Direct merchants.
Acquirer Merchant ID for transaction processing.
Toggles the visibility of the product catalogue during the Web KYB process. Defaults to false if not specified.
Settlement frequency for the merchant payments.
Array of service provider configurations for the merchant.
Unique identifier of the service provider.
Whether to deduct applicable transaction fees.
Fee structure for the service provider.
Array of adjustment types for the fee structure.
Specifies the billing plan based on which transaction cost is calculated for a merchant. If more than one billing plan exists it is mandatory to send in this value.
Configuration for payment methods to be enabled for the merchant.
Card payment method configuration. Can be a boolean to enable/disable or an object with specific settings.
American Express payment method configuration. Can be a boolean to enable/disable or an object with specific settings.
American Express Merchant ID. Required when configuring AMEX payment method.
Swedish mobile payment method configuration. Can be a boolean to enable/disable or an object with specific settings.
Optional Swish number for the payment method configuration.
Buy now, pay later payment method configuration. Can be a boolean to enable/disable or an object with specific settings.
Klarna Merchant ID for the payment method configuration.
Klarna account username. Required when configuring KLARNA payment method.
Klarna account password. Required when configuring KLARNA payment method.
B2B invoice payment method configuration. Can be a boolean to enable/disable or an object with specific settings.
Billing plan for B2B invoice payments.
Client ID for B2B invoice payment method.
Client secret key for B2B invoice payment method.
Account to account transfer payment method configuration. Can be a boolean to enable/disable or an object with specific settings.
Merchant ID for account to account transfers.
Norwegian mobile payment method. Set to true to enable Vipps payment method.
Danish mobile payment method. Set to true to enable MobilePay payment method.
A set of products that are pre-selected by you for the merchant. These products are shipped as part of the onboarding process without further input from the merchant.
Specifies the billing plan for a merchant. This allows you to set up a plan based on the monthly price for a terminal and the duration in months. The specific plan details are provided by Surfboard.
This will be the redirect URL from the web kyb page, once the application is submitted the user can choose to redirect to the provided URL.
Set as true when you need the short URL for the web kyb page. Default is set to false.
Input the store details if you also want to create a store for the merchant.
Contact number of the store in code and number format.
A string of numbers ranging from 0-9 with a length of 5-15 characters.
City where the store is located.
Two-letter ISO country code in uppercase, representing the country where the store is located.
In order to start accepting online payments you need to create an online store which requires some additional properties such as the following.
This is the web-shop URL of the merchant.
This is the URL of the payment page. This is required for integrating online payments using SDK mode.
This is the URL of the T&C of the merchant’s web-shop, it has to contain the refund policy.
This is the URL of the privacy policy of the merchant.
Use this param to pre fill the merchant application
These details helps us understand when payments are accepted for the merchant.
If they are a seasonal merchant, open only on select seasons.
If they are a seasonal merchant, mention the months they will be open for payments. e.g., Seasonal shops open only during january and map can be mentioned as [1,5]
If merchant will be accepting payments all year round.
Mention if merchant stores are open all night.
Provide a reason as to why they are open all night. Mandatory when 'isStoreOpenAtNight' is set to true.
Gift card details of the merchant if any.
Estimated amount spent using gift cards per year.
Average validity of gift card provided by merchant.
Details of pre payment option provided by merchant
Average delivery time in days taken after pre payment.
Percentage of sales revenue generated via pre payment.
Sales information with respect to the merchant.
Estimated amount of sales per year.
Estimated amount of sales per day.
Denotes how often transactions are made under merchant. Possible values are 'DAILY', 'WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY', 'YEARLY'
Average number of sales per day. Mandatory when 'estimatedFrequencyOfTransactions' is set to 'DAILY'
This is the applicationId that you will use to track the status of the create merchant request.
This is the link for the KYC session created for the merchant.
For PF partners, merchant is created and the merchantId is returned.
For PF partners, store is created and the storeId is returned.
Short URL of the web kyb page if generateShortLink is set to true.
A message that describes the status of the request.

stepDescription: Check the status of the application
tags: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'
The Application ID of the respective merchant returned by the Create Merchant API.

This is the link for the KYC session created for the merchant, which can be fetched for applications in state 'APPLICATION_INITIATED' or 'APPLICATION_PENDING_INFORMATION'. This URL will be returned for ISV(Independent Software Vendors) partners only.
Describes the status of the application. The possible values are:
The application has been created, and the Web KYB URL has been generated. The merchant is now required to fill in necessary information through the Web KYB.
The merchant has finished filling in the Web KYB and submitted it for processing.
The application is still under processing, and additional data is required from the merchant.
Both the merchant signatories and UBOs have signed the application.
The application has been processed and rejected.
The application has been processed and accepted. It is now pending merchant creation and onboarding.
Each application is valid for 30 days. If there is no action during these 30 days, the KYB is treated as session expired.
The merchant account has been created. At this stage, the Merchant ID and Store ID will be returned in the status call.
Merchant ID of the created merchant is returned when application status is 'MERCHANT_CREATED'
Store ID of the store created under the merchant is returned when application status is 'MERCHANT_CREATED'
A message that describes the status of the request.

stepDescription: Registering the instore terminals
tags: Usually, the registrationIdentifier is a 6-digit code found upon starting the terminal. For the SurfPad and Printer, you need to use the serial number from the back of the device.
Assign a name for your terminal. An optional param to identify a terminal for the user.
Terminal ID of the registered terminal.
Represents the current status of the terminal. The possible values are:
The terminal has been successfully registered.
A message that describes the status of the terminal registration.

stepDescription: Registering the online terminals
tags: Online payments can be carried out using any of these following modes.

In this mode, the payment page and all associated configuration is managed by Surfboard. This is the simplest mode to integrate as all that is required as an integrating partner is to redirect the customer to the payment page. The payment page needs to be the top level frame in any given context.
With SelfHostedPage, Surfboard renders the fields in your page. This provides you with maximum customization as to render the layout any way you want.
Set this type if you want to accept Merchant Initiated Transactions (MIT).
Terminal ID of the registered terminal.
For SelfHostedPage terminals use this key to initialize your Online SDK.
Represents the current status of the terminal. The possible values are:
The terminal has been successfully registered.
The terminal was previously registered but has now been removed from the system.
A message that describes the status of the terminal registration.

stepDescription: Configure settings for all terminals of a merchant
tags: Auto sleep time for the terminal in seconds. Terminal will sleep if no transactions are received in this time period. Only applies if the terminal is running on battery.

Scheduled restart time for the terminal in HH:MM format. The default is 02:00am according to the device's timezone. It has a 1 hour restart window. For example, if set to 02:00am, restart could occur anytime between 02:00am and 02:59am. It is essential for the device to restart at least once every 24 hours. This might be particularly important for businesses that operate beyond the default restart time.
Preferred network for the terminal. It can be either 'WIFI' or 'GSM'.

Determines how the amount is displayed on the terminal. It can be either '0' or '1'. When set to '1', the terminal will always display the minor units, even for whole numbers (e.g., 29 will be displayed as 29.00). When set to 0, the terminal will not include minor units for whole numbers.
Two letter ISO language code, in lowercase, representing the language to be displayed in the terminal. Can be 'en', 'fi', 'da' , 'se'.
Determines whether the status bar is displayed on the terminal. If set 'TRUE', the terminal will display the status bar.This is applicable to SurfPad terminals.
Time delay in milliseconds before the card is read.
Set this to determine how receipts are showcased on the terminal.
The preferred GSM operator identified by a numeric code.
Indicates whether the POS system should open automatically after reboot. Possible values are 'enabled' or 'disabled'.
Determines if the refund lock screen feature is enabled on the device. This is only available for android terminals.
The PIN code used to authorize refunds on the screen. This is only available for android terminals.
The duration (in milliseconds) before the screen times out due to inactivity.
The interval (in milliseconds) before the device automatically goes to sleep.
Timeout of the receipt screen on hardware terminals.
A message that describes the status of the request.

Two letter ISO language code, in lowercase, representing the language to be displayed in the terminal. Can be 'en', 'fi', 'da' , 'se'.
Determines whether the status bar is displayed on the terminal. If set 'TRUE', the terminal will display the status bar. This is applicable to SurfPad terminals.
Time delay in milliseconds before the card is read.
Set this to determine how receipts are showcased on the terminal.
The preferred GSM operator identified by a numeric code.
Indicates whether the POS system should open automatically after reboot. Possible values are 'enabled' or 'disabled'.
Determines if the refund lock screen feature is enabled on the device. This is only available for android terminals.
The PIN code used to authorize refunds on the screen. This is only available for android terminals.
The duration (in milliseconds) before the screen times out due to inactivity.
The interval (in milliseconds) before the device automatically goes to sleep.
Timeout of the receipt screen on hardware terminals.
A message that describes the status of the request.

tags: Auto sleep time for the terminal in seconds. Terminal will sleep if no transactions are received in this time period. Only applies if the terminal is running on battery.

stepDescription: Check the status of the order
tags: Status of the request, indicated as either 'SUCCESS' or 'ERROR'.
Describes the status of the order. The possible values are:
An order has been created and is waiting for payment processing.
The payment has been completed for the order.
The payment attempt has been cancelled for the order.
A partial payment was completed for the order
Retrieves the status of all payments related to the order.
Describes the status of the payment. The possible values are:
A payment attempt has been initiated.
The payment attempt has been cancelled.
Indicates the reason of the payment failure if it is available. You can refer to Concepts → Payments for a complete list of payment failure reasons.
Amount involved with the payment attempt.
An array of successful Payment Id(s) related to an order. Payment Ids are available once order reaches 'PAYMENT_COMPLETED' or 'PARTIAL_PAYMENT_COMPLETED' state.
Retrieves all transaction details for the order. This information is only available when orderStatus is 'PAYMENT_COMPLETED' or 'PARTIAL_PAYMENT_COMPLETED'
Merchant ID of the merchant who performed the transaction.
Store ID of the store in which the transaction was performed
Terminal ID of the checkout or payment terminal from which the transaction was performed.
Order ID of the order for which the transaction was performed.
Displays the Retrieval Reference Number(RRN) of the transaction. RRN is a key to uniquely identify a card transaction based on the ISO 8583 standard.
Amount involved in the transaction, either received or refunded by the merchant, in the minor unit of the currency. For example, 10 SEK is 1000 in amount.
Three digit numeric code representing the currency of the transaction.
The method in which the transaction was carried out. Payment method supported currently is 'CARD’.
Type of the transaction. It can be either 'purchase' or 'return’.
Specifies a short summary of the order. Usually contains the initial line items included in the order.
Timestamp of the transaction in ISO 8601 format represented as 'YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ'.
A boolean value that denotes if the transaction has been voided or not.
Interchange domain of the transaction. It can be Domestic | Intraregional-EEA | Interregional-Non-EEA.
Denotes the card category, which can be Consumer | Commercial.
Denotes the card type, whether it is Debit, Credit or Prepaid.

The fee collected for each transaction. Fee associated with your settlements are determined by the billing plan you have chosen. These costs may include transaction fees, processing charges, and any additional fees specified in your subscription plan.
Denotes the status of the settlement.
Settlement processed. Waiting on amount to be credited to merchant's bank account.
Error occurred during the settlement process.
Payout is the final amount Surfboard credits to the merchant's bank account. This represents the merchant's actual earnings, which are then transferred to their bank account.
Reference Id for the payout which includes this transaction.
Date of the settlement for the transaction. Date in ISO string format.
The last four digits of the PAN (Primary Account Number) from the card used for the transaction.
The designated label of the card brand for the transaction, often referred to as the AID (Application Identifier) Label.
Indicates the mode of entry for the card during the transaction, which is based on EMV specifications. This determines whether the card was used in a contact or contactless manner.
Specifies the payment application used in the transaction. Usually present when cards with both Debit and Credit facility are used.
Specifies the additional results if the transaction underwent EMV terminal verification.
Specifies the Application Identifier (AID) associated with the card used for the transaction.
A two-character response code of the transaction. For all approved transactions, this code is '00'.
Indicates the Card Holder Verification Method (CVM) used in the transaction.
Specifies the cvmMethod applied for the provided CARD transaction.
Indicates the authentication mode of the payment. This can be either 'ISSUER' or 'CARD'.
Denotes the brand of card used for the transaction. This is an optional normalized field available only for CARD payments. Eg., VISA, MASTERCARD, AMEX.
A message that describes the status of the request.

Reference Id for the payout which includes this transaction.
Date of the settlement for the transaction. Date in ISO string format.
